physicalDetails.detail: Arrives dust to the knees on a lathered gray mare, one glove gone, braid coming apart, dispatch tube at her hip, ink staining two fingers, and one hard-won stiffness in the right knee after the ride south.
Chapter 24 recasts Vereesa in public duty mode: formal, controlled, and visibly forcing sister-grief under protocol while she reads the Convocation's disavowal into a hostile courtroom.

voiceMarkers.detail: Opens in rigid chamber register — title first, law first, punishment first — then lets the formal cadence crack into younger-sister anger, grief, and direct plea.
The family language is the striking marker: 'Lady Sun,' 'Come home,' and the refusal to let Sylvanas answer her only as commander to captain.

beliefs.belief: If Sylvanas returns now, family, city, and name might still be salvageable; if she refuses in public, desertion becomes real and perhaps irreversible.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Active and emotionally sincere in chapter 19, though the chapter makes clear Sylvanas no longer shares the frame.

beliefs.belief: Lor'themar is spending himself to keep this mission alive and the cost at home is no longer abstract chamber friction.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed by Vereesa's own report in chapter 19.

goalsAtChapterEnd.active: Stay in the city after the disavowal and keep whatever thread remains between Silvermoon, Sylvanas, and the worsening blade crisis.
goalsAtChapterEnd.underlying: Refuse to let the public severing of faction cover become the final form of the sister bond.

emotionalStateAtChapterEnd.state: Grief-locked, formal on the surface, and unwilling to leave even after being made the mouthpiece for her own sister's abandonment.

decisionsThisChapter.detail: Rides hard from Silvermoon carrying the Convocation's sealed recall herself instead of letting the order arrive by clerk or distant relay.
Reports the true political cost of Lor'themar's defense rather than softening it into chamber abstraction.
Accepts the refusal once Sylvanas states it plainly and turns back for home carrying the answer without open disobedience.
Reads the Convocation's public disavowal into the tribunal record and then chooses to remain in Lordaeron instead of treating the reading as the end of her obligation.

abilitiesDemonstrated.ability: Hard-distance riding under pressure; reaches camp fast enough that horse, gear, and body all show what she spent on the road.
Can carry both official register and personal wound in the same confrontation, shifting between them as the scene demands.
Can make ceremonial denunciation sound like duty rather than conviction, preserving room for later action even while speaking the party line.

howOthersAddressHer.speaker: Sylvanas Windrunner
howOthersAddressHer.pattern: Uses Vereesa's given name until the confrontation hardens, then answers as commander to captain.

howSheAddressesOthers.pattern: Begins with 'Ranger-General,' shifts to direct sister-address and 'Lady Sun,' then lands on rank again at departure.
Does not address Jaina directly on page; reads her instead as the other woman not coming back.

bodyLanguagePatterns.detail: Holds the sealed vellum out level when Sylvanas refuses to take it, arm steady long after the ride should have shaken her.
Flinches once when Sylvanas names the tactic behind the messenger choice, folds the recall with careful control after the refusal, and does not look back once she turns for Silvermoon.

knowledge.detail: Knows the Convocation has suspended Sylvanas's foreign-deployment authority and means to treat refusal as desertion under military law.
Carries Lor'themar's political report: a token mobilization, relay support to the northern harbors, and the near-total depletion of what leverage he still had.
Leaves camp knowing Jaina is not returning to Dalaran either and that north has become the shared road of the remaining formation.
Now knows Sylvanas and Jaina have been convicted, that Frostmourne remains the more urgent danger, and that Lor'themar has reached the city with independent corroboration of the blade's wrongness.

arc.lie: Home can still be preserved if the right person chooses family and rank soon enough.
arc.truth: By chapter 19 the road has already outrun private rescue; love now survives only as what she can carry back from the refusal.
arc.currentPosition: Chapter 24 turns Vereesa from messenger of exile into public witness of it. She is still carrying institutional language, but she no longer lets that language carry her away from the city or from Sylvanas's danger.
